author: @stevenickolls8016
text: Outdoor lights can become a nuisance to neighbours with the unwanted glow if left on all night and even when the owner has gone on holiday. Solar being cost free after installation can end up forgotten leading to the issue I mention. Then there's the unintended effects on birds, plants and insects. If you have the lights on just when youre outside enjoying them then go ahead just remember others might not share your view.

author: @baronbullshyster2996
text: Putting the board on the wall with the stand off is very helpful as it give somewhere for the spiders to live😊❤

author: @pendarischneider
text:
Two things I believe to be true about lead-acid batteries —
1. Hydrolysis only happens when the battery is overcharged, i.e., boiling off the excess energy to save the battery (a good charge controller should produce no hydrogen); and,
2. Best use of lead acid batteries is not cycling below 80% full charge to maximise battery life, so 5x batteries needed for long life of a given capacity (cost benefit analysis needed!).
Hope this is a useful addition, and I very much enjoyed your no nonsense "just do it" approach with good (safe) information. Thank you.

author: @RobertSchmitt-u7l
text:
I love his spin on solar. 
I think theres alot of wise choices and its executed very well. 
It would be great to have one thick 1pcb ALL absolutely all needed circuits, connectos, screens, buttons inverter, heatsink mppt STRTA. 
For a specific voltage to make it simple hey 12v, 48v 120v option, 240 combined. 
IF it even had a bms system on it and its mounted to the literal lifepo4 cells, I would entertain that idea. 
I see no reason why not, the heat from the inverter can be used in cold. 
It has a bms stage, a mppt stage, an inverter stage, screen for settings on bms, and solar, and inverter. If its a known industry standard cell, many options can be simplified or even ANALOG! The 2 toroids, and inductors and heat sinks can all be mounted in center, the solar on one side, dc, and the output on the other configurable to a 120 or 240 grid. Probably not grid tied. As when solar panels are not throughputting power, they are also supposedly not degrading... 
Low loss
Low copper use
Use toroids (or even amorphous core REALLY)  in paralell and or series to keep it compact, and versatile easy universal seleciton. 
Ultra ruggedized top quality components, all integrated. Even,,, you know surface mount super efficent mosfets!

I would love to have one designed by a company willing to invest the intricate layout, but also, smart reliable use of component. 
AND simple and most definetly all on ONE board. 

If the cells are the needed size, 300ah or 600 ah options, i think for home use the 600ah would be bullet proof. 
But sooo much can be done with 300ah cells too. With house efficiency, insulation, and proper heat pumps, even geothermal, the smaller battery can be coupled with a very wise way to construct a home. 

Argh, I wish i had pure reign and power over a design company, just tell them to do this!!! Hha ha!
